articles.title: Joey Barton Says He Did Not Hurt Someone at a Golf Club
articles.summary: Joey Barton is a former football player. He is in trouble because he is accused of hurting a man at a golf club in Merseyside. The man is Kevin Lynch, who used to be a football manager. Joey Barton says he did not do it. He went to court by video from prison. Another man, Gary O'Grady, is also accused. They were drinking with Kevin Lynch before the problem happened. Kevin Lynch got a bad injury to his eye. Joey Barton will have a trial in September to decide if he is guilty. He played for many football teams like Manchester City. This story is about the law and sports.

articles.title: Doctors Go on Strike in England
articles.summary: Doctors in England have started a six-day strike because they want better pay. These doctors, called resident doctors, work in hospitals and make up almost half of the medical staff. The strike began on Tuesday morning and is causing big problems for hospitals. Many appointments and treatments are being canceled. Senior doctors are helping in emergencies, but regular care is affected. The government and doctors' union talked but could not agree. Patients with emergencies should still call 999 or 111. The government says the strike costs a lot of money, but doctors say their pay has not kept up with rising prices. Both sides are sorry for the trouble but blame each other for the strike.

articles.title: Warmest Weather Coming to the UK
articles.summary: The UK will have the warmest weather of the year this week. On Tuesday and Wednesday, temperatures will be very high, reaching up to 25°C in some places like south-east England. This is much warmer than usual for early April. The weather will be sunny and dry, making it feel like summer. People can enjoy outdoor activities. But on Thursday, rain will come from the north-west, and it will get colder again. The warm air is coming from Europe, pushed by a breeze. This makes pollen levels rise, which might affect people with allergies. It's a good time to play outside but remember to stay safe in the sun.

articles.title: Astronauts Fly Far from Earth and See the Moon Up Close
articles.summary: Four astronauts on a NASA mission called Artemis II are coming back to Earth after a big adventure. They flew around the Moon and went further away from Earth than any people have ever gone before. While they were behind the Moon, they lost contact with Earth for 40 minutes, but then they could talk again. They saw a total eclipse of the Sun when the Moon blocked its light. The astronauts took pictures and drew sketches of the Moon's surface, especially the far side that we never see from Earth. They broke a record set by Apollo 13 in 1970. The President called to congratulate them, and they asked to name some craters on the Moon. It was an exciting trip to explore space.

articles.title: President Trump's Threats to Iran: What Experts Say
articles.summary: President Donald Trump said he might destroy bridges and power plants in Iran. Experts in military law think this could be a war crime. A war crime is when actions in war hurt civilians too much or are not fair. The experts say that if the power plants are not military targets, or if many civilians get hurt, it could break international law. Some people from the United Nations and Democrats in Congress agree. The president often talks big but does not always do what he says. He gave Iran a deadline to open a waterway called the Strait of Hormuz. A U.N. spokesman warned that attacking such things is not allowed under the rules of war.

articles.title: Farmers Worry About Higher Food Prices Due to War
articles.summary: A war is making it hard for farmers to get fertilizer, which is a special plant food that helps crops grow. About one-third of the world's fertilizer goes through a place called the Strait of Hormuz, but the war has closed it, causing shortages and higher prices. This is happening during spring, when farmers plant seeds, so they might not have enough fertilizer for their crops. As a result, food prices could go up, and there might be less food available. Farmers are worried because these costs could hurt their farms. The U.S. Agriculture Secretary says most farmers bought fertilizer last fall, but some still need help to avoid going broke. This problem affects everyone because food is important for our daily lives.
